Selected `gemini-flash`'s description 'Recepta Varia.'. Visual inspection shows 'Recepta' is more accurate than `gpt-5-mini`'s 'Recepte'. Both agreed on header type and no amounts.
This row was found by `gpt-5-mini` but missed by `gemini-flash`. Visual inspection confirms its presence as a distinct header (year) in the left margin. No amounts present.

rows.description: Janry 26. Received of Mr Bonsey by Bill Due Mid: 1755.
rows.notes: Description manually corrected. `gemini-flash` correctly transcribed 'Janry' and 'Bonsey' but incorrectly prepended '1756.'. `gpt-5-mini` had 'Jany' and 'Bonney'. Amounts (100 0 0) were agreed by both and are clear visually.
rows.english_description: Payment received from Mr Bonsey by bill, for a sum that was due mid-1755.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment received from Mr Bonsey by bill, for a sum that was due mid-1755, amounting to £100 0s 0d.

rows.description: May 4. Receivd of Ditto due Xms 1755.
rows.notes: Selected `gemini-flash`'s description ('Ditto due Xms 1755') as visually accurate, contra `gpt-5-mini`'s incorrect 'Potts due Jan 1755.'. Amounts (100 0 0) were agreed by both and are clear visually.
rows.english_description: Payment received from Mr Bonsey, representing an amount due at Christmas 1755.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment received from Mr Bonsey, representing an amount due at Christmas 1755, amounting to £100 0s 0d.

rows.description: — of Medenham Estate renewed at St Thom: 1754. yr whole Fine 100£: Coll: Share 33-6-8 the clut share 4th part —
rows.notes: Selected `gemini-flash` for the much more complete and accurate description and amounts. Visual inspection confirms the ledger's pounds column is empty for this row, shillings is '8', and pence is '6'. `gpt-5-mini` incorrectly read amounts as 8 pounds, 6 shillings, 8 pence and significantly abbreviated the description.
rows.english_description: College's share of the fine on renewal of the Medenham estate at St Thomas 1754 (renewal fine share).
rows.english_description_with_amount: College's share of the fine on renewal of the Medenham estate at St Thomas 1754, recorded as £0 8s 6d.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt of an Exchequer annuity due at Lady Day 1756, amounting to £62 10s 0d.

rows.description: July 26. Recievd of Mr Williams Jervis yr ch: share for ye sale of Timber at Medenham

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Mr Williams Jervis representing his share of proceeds from the sale of timber at Medenham, amounting to £15 19s 1½d.

rows.notes: Selected `gemini-flash`'s description for greater accuracy ('Aug:', 'Deficiency', 'Battelds:', 'Cart') contra `gpt-5-mini`'s 'Augt', 'deficiency', 'battes', 'cost'. Amounts (0 10 0) were agreed by both and are clear visually.
